1	"""Regression for the April 2026 m4a founder upload.
2	
3	A 194.8 MB .m4a landed in the Codex with ``textContent=NULL``. The
4	pre-Wave-1 bridge raised ``RuntimeError("Upload ... has no textContent")``
5	and marked the upload failed. The Wave-1 refactor routes these rows
6	through a MIME dispatcher that picks the audio extractor instead.
7	
8	This test pins that behaviour: an Upload row with ``mimeType='audio/mp4'``
9	and ``textContent=NULL`` must extract claims successfully, not raise
10	about the missing column. The extractor itself is stubbed so the test
11	runs in under a second without loading faster-whisper weights (and
12	without depending on ffmpeg being installed for fixture synthesis).
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	from pathlib import Path
18	
19	import pytest
20	
21	from noosphere.codex_bridge import ingest_from_codex
22	from noosphere.extractors import audio_extractor
23	from noosphere.extractors.base import ExtractedText, ExtractionFailed
24	
25	
26	def _dummy_m4a(tmp_path: Path) -> Path:
27	    """Write a few bytes with an .m4a extension. The stubbed extractor
28	    never parses them — we just need a real file for fetch_upload_content
29	    to read."""
30	    p = tmp_path / "founders_chat.m4a"
31	    p.write_bytes(b"\x00\x00\x00\x20ftypM4A dummy regression bytes")
32	    return p
33	
34	
35	def test_m4a_upload_no_longer_raises_textcontent_error(
36	    fake_codex_db, codex_sqlite_url, upload_factory,
37	    monkeypatch, tmp_path,
38	):
39	    """Canary for the whole Wave-1 audio-ingest fix.
40	
41	    Before: textContent=NULL + mime=audio/mp4 → RuntimeError.
42	    After: audio dispatcher runs Whisper (stubbed here) and yields claims.
43	    """
44	    dummy = _dummy_m4a(tmp_path)
45	    uid = upload_factory(
46	        mime="audio/mp4",
47	        text=None,
48	        file_path=str(dummy),
49	        file_size=dummy.stat().st_size,
50	        original_name="founders_chat.m4a",
51	        title="Founder chat",
52	    )
53	
54	    # Stub the audio extractor so we don't load faster-whisper weights.
55	    # The transcript is chosen so the naive claim extractor picks it up
56	    # (>= 40 chars, contains a verb, not a question).
57	    def _fake_extract(self, content):
58	        assert content.mime == "audio/mp4"
59	        return ExtractedText(
60	            text="the purpose of the school is inquiry not credentialing.",
61	            source_format="audio/mp4",
62	            extraction_method="faster-whisper",
63	        )
64	
65	    monkeypatch.setattr(audio_extractor.AudioExtractor, "extract", _fake_extract)
66	
67	    result = ingest_from_codex(
68	        upload_id=uid,
69	        use_llm=False,
70	        dry_run=True,
71	        codex_db_url=codex_sqlite_url,
72	    )
73	
74	    assert result.num_claims_extracted > 0, (
75	        "audio upload should yield at least one claim from the stubbed transcript; "
76	        "a zero count means the m4a regression path is still broken."
77	    )
78	    assert result.dry_run is True
79	    assert result.mode == "naive"
80	
81	
82	def test_audio_upload_does_not_error_on_missing_textcontent(
83	    fake_codex_db, codex_sqlite_url, upload_factory,
84	    monkeypatch, tmp_path,
85	):
86	    """End-to-end (non-dry-run) variant. The pre-Wave-1 bridge ended in
87	    ``status='failed'`` for this row shape; the refactor must land it at
88	    ``status='ingested'`` with a populated ``extractionMethod``."""
89	    dummy = _dummy_m4a(tmp_path)
90	    uid = upload_factory(
91	        mime="audio/mp4",
92	        text=None,

96	
97	    def _fake_extract(self, content):
98	        return ExtractedText(
99	            text=(
100	                "the purpose of the school is inquiry not credentialing, "
101	                "and this sentence exists to satisfy the naive extractor."
102	            ),
103	            source_format="audio/mp4",
104	            extraction_method="faster-whisper",
105	        )
106	
107	    monkeypatch.setattr(audio_extractor.AudioExtractor, "extract", _fake_extract)
108	
109	    result = ingest_from_codex(
110	        upload_id=uid,
111	        use_llm=False,

113	        codex_db_url=codex_sqlite_url,

136	    uid = upload_factory(
137	        mime="audio/mp4",
138	        text=None,
139	        file_path=str(dummy),
140	        file_size=dummy.stat().st_size,
141	    )
142	
143	    def _boom(self, content):
144	        raise ExtractionFailed("whisper weights missing and no OPENAI_API_KEY set")
145	
146	    monkeypatch.setattr(audio_extractor.AudioExtractor, "extract", _boom)
147	
148	    with pytest.raises(ExtractionFailed):
149	        ingest_from_codex(

"""Regression for the April 2026 m4a founder upload.

A 194.8 MB .m4a landed in the Codex with ``textContent=NULL``. The
pre-Wave-1 bridge raised ``RuntimeError("Upload ... has no textContent")``
and marked the upload failed. The Wave-1 refactor routes these rows
through a MIME dispatcher that picks the audio extractor instead.

This test pins that behaviour: an Upload row with ``mimeType='audio/mp4'``
and ``textContent=NULL`` must extract claims successfully, not raise
about the missing column. The extractor itself is stubbed so the test
runs in under a second without loading faster-whisper weights (and
without depending on ffmpeg being installed for fixture synthesis).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from noosphere.codex_bridge import ingest_from_codex
from noosphere.extractors import audio_extractor
from noosphere.extractors.base import ExtractedText, ExtractionFailed


def _dummy_m4a(tmp_path: Path) -> Path:
    """Write a few bytes with an .m4a extension. The stubbed extractor
    never parses them — we just need a real file for fetch_upload_content
    to read."""
    p = tmp_path / "founders_chat.m4a"
    p.write_bytes(b"\x00\x00\x00\x20ftypM4A dummy regression bytes")
    return p


def test_m4a_upload_no_longer_raises_textcontent_error(
    fake_codex_db, codex_sqlite_url, upload_factory,
    monkeypatch, tmp_path,
):
    """Canary for the whole Wave-1 audio-ingest fix.

    Before: textContent=NULL + mime=audio/mp4 → RuntimeError.
    After: audio dispatcher runs Whisper (stubbed here) and yields claims.
    """
    dummy = _dummy_m4a(tmp_path)
    uid = upload_factory(
        mime="audio/mp4",
        text=None,
        file_path=str(dummy),
        file_size=dummy.stat().st_size,
        original_name="founders_chat.m4a",
        title="Founder chat",
    )

    # Stub the audio extractor so we don't load faster-whisper weights.
    # The transcript is chosen so the naive claim extractor picks it up
    # (>= 40 chars, contains a verb, not a question).
    def _fake_extract(self, content):
        assert content.mime == "audio/mp4"
        return ExtractedText(
            text="the purpose of the school is inquiry not credentialing.",
            source_format="audio/mp4",
            extraction_method="faster-whisper",
        )

    monkeypatch.setattr(audio_extractor.AudioExtractor, "extract", _fake_extract)

    result = ingest_from_codex(
        upload_id=uid,
        use_llm=False,
        dry_run=True,
        codex_db_url=codex_sqlite_url,
    )

    assert result.num_claims_extracted > 0, (
        "audio upload should yield at least one claim from the stubbed transcript; "
        "a zero count means the m4a regression path is still broken."
    )
    assert result.dry_run is True
    assert result.mode == "naive"


def test_audio_upload_does_not_error_on_missing_textcontent(
    fake_codex_db, codex_sqlite_url, upload_factory,
    monkeypatch, tmp_path,
):
    """End-to-end (non-dry-run) variant. The pre-Wave-1 bridge ended in
    ``status='failed'`` for this row shape; the refactor must land it at
    ``status='ingested'`` with a populated ``extractionMethod``."""
    dummy = _dummy_m4a(tmp_path)
    uid = upload_factory(
        mime="audio/mp4",
        text=None,
        file_path=str(dummy),
        file_size=dummy.stat().st_size,
    )

    def _fake_extract(self, content):
        return ExtractedText(
            text=(
                "the purpose of the school is inquiry not credentialing, "
                "and this sentence exists to satisfy the naive extractor."
            ),
            source_format="audio/mp4",
            extraction_method="faster-whisper",
        )

    monkeypatch.setattr(audio_extractor.AudioExtractor, "extract", _fake_extract)

    result = ingest_from_codex(
        upload_id=uid,
        use_llm=False,
        dry_run=False,
        codex_db_url=codex_sqlite_url,
    )

    row = fake_codex_db.execute(
        'SELECT status, "errorMessage", "extractionMethod" FROM "Upload" WHERE id = ?',
        (uid,),
    ).fetchone()
    assert row["status"] == "ingested", (
        f"expected status=ingested but got {row['status']!r}; "
        f"errorMessage={row['errorMessage']!r}"
    )
    assert row["errorMessage"] is None
    assert row["extractionMethod"] == "faster-whisper"
    assert result.num_claims_extracted >= 1


def test_failure_message_no_longer_mentions_textcontent(
    fake_codex_db, codex_sqlite_url, upload_factory,
    monkeypatch, tmp_path,
):
    """Even on an extractor failure path the error message must NOT say
    "has no textContent" — that was the specific pre-Wave-1 phrasing."""
    dummy = _dummy_m4a(tmp_path)
    uid = upload_factory(
        mime="audio/mp4",
        text=None,
        file_path=str(dummy),
        file_size=dummy.stat().st_size,
    )

    def _boom(self, content):
        raise ExtractionFailed("whisper weights missing and no OPENAI_API_KEY set")

    monkeypatch.setattr(audio_extractor.AudioExtractor, "extract", _boom)

    with pytest.raises(ExtractionFailed):
        ingest_from_codex(
            upload_id=uid,
            use_llm=False,
            dry_run=False,
            codex_db_url=codex_sqlite_url,
        )

    row = fake_codex_db.execute(
        'SELECT status, "errorMessage" FROM "Upload" WHERE id = ?',
        (uid,),
    ).fetchone()
    assert row["status"] == "failed"
    err = row["errorMessage"] or ""
    # The pre-Wave-1 phrasing that this test is a guard against:
    assert "textContent" not in err
    assert "has no textContent" not in err
    # The underlying extractor's reason must surface somewhere so the
    # founder can see WHY it failed (not just "it failed"). The dispatcher
    # prefixes it with extraction_failed: so the dashboard can colour it.
    assert "extraction_failed" in err
    assert "whisper weights missing" in err

"""MIME-family coverage for the ingest pipeline.

The parameterized matrix exercises every supported content type (text,
audio, PDF) and several deliberately unsupported ones (zip, image, empty
mime). Adding a new extractor later means adding one row here — the
invariant is visible in the test list.

All binary extractors are stubbed so the whole suite runs in under a
second and needs no models, no ffmpeg, no reportlab, no ocrmypdf.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from noosphere.codex_bridge import ingest_from_codex
from noosphere.extractors import audio_extractor, pdf_extractor
from noosphere.extractors.base import ExtractedText, ExtractionFailed, UnsupportedMimeType


_AUDIO_TRANSCRIPT = (
    "the purpose of the school is inquiry, and this sentence "
    "exists to trip the naive extractor's length + verb checks."
)
_PDF_TEXT = (
    "the argument of this paper is that schools should pursue inquiry "
    "over credentialing, and this sentence is long enough to qualify."
)


@pytest.fixture
def stub_binary_extractors(monkeypatch):
    """Stub AudioExtractor.extract + PdfExtractor.extract so the matrix
    never loads faster-whisper or pypdf. The stubs return deterministic
    text that survives the naive claim extractor."""

    def _audio(self, content):
        return ExtractedText(
            text=_AUDIO_TRANSCRIPT,
            source_format=content.mime,
            extraction_method="faster-whisper-stub",
        )

    def _pdf(self, content):
        return ExtractedText(
            text=_PDF_TEXT,
            source_format="application/pdf",
            extraction_method="pypdf-stub",
        )

    monkeypatch.setattr(audio_extractor.AudioExtractor, "extract", _audio)
    monkeypatch.setattr(pdf_extractor.PdfExtractor, "extract", _pdf)


def _dummy_binary(tmp_path: Path, name: str, head: bytes = b"") -> Path:
    p = tmp_path / name
    # A few plausible leading bytes plus some filler so fetch_upload_content
    # has something to read. No extractor reads past this — stubs ignore it,
    # and the unsupported-mime branch fails before parsing.
    p.write_bytes(head + b"\x00" * 64)
    return p


# (label, mime, textContent, filename|None, expected_outcome)
#   textContent == None → the Upload's file_path is used and the extractor runs
#   expected_outcome == "ingested" → pipeline completes, status=ingested
#   expected_outcome == "failed:<reason-fragment>" → pipeline marks failed
MATRIX: list[tuple[str, str, str | None, str | None, str]] = [
    ("text_plain",       "text/plain",        "hello world",      None,            "ingested"),
    ("text_markdown",    "text/markdown",     "# hi\n\nbody",     None,            "ingested"),
    ("application_json", "application/json",  '{"a": 1}',         None,            "ingested"),
    ("audio_mp4",        "audio/mp4",         None,               "talk.m4a",      "ingested"),
    ("audio_mpeg",       "audio/mpeg",        None,               "talk.mp3",      "ingested"),
    ("audio_wav",        "audio/wav",         None,               "talk.wav",      "ingested"),
    ("application_pdf",  "application/pdf",   None,               "paper.pdf",     "ingested"),
    ("application_zip",  "application/zip",   None,               "bundle.zip",    "failed:unsupported_mime"),
    ("image_png",        "image/png",         None,               "photo.png",     "failed:unsupported_mime"),
    ("empty_mime",       "",                  None,               "mystery.bin",   "failed:unsupported_mime"),
]


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "label,mime,text,filename,expected",
    MATRIX,
    ids=[c[0] for c in MATRIX],
)
def test_mime_matrix(
    label, mime, text, filename, expected,
    fake_codex_db, codex_sqlite_url, upload_factory,
    stub_binary_extractors, tmp_path,
):
    file_path: str | None = None
    file_size = 0
    if filename is not None:
        path = _dummy_binary(tmp_path, filename)
        file_path = str(path)
        file_size = path.stat().st_size

    uid = upload_factory(
        mime=mime,
        text=text,
        file_path=file_path,
        file_size=file_size,
        original_name=filename or "inline.txt",
    )

    if expected == "ingested":
        result = ingest_from_codex(
            upload_id=uid,
            use_llm=False,
            dry_run=False,
            codex_db_url=codex_sqlite_url,
        )
        assert result.dry_run is False
        row = fake_codex_db.execute(
            'SELECT status, "errorMessage" FROM "Upload" WHERE id = ?',
            (uid,),
        ).fetchone()
        assert row["status"] == "ingested", (
            f"{label}: expected status=ingested, got {row['status']!r} "
            f"(errorMessage={row['errorMessage']!r})"
        )
    elif expected.startswith("failed:"):
        fragment = expected.split(":", 1)[1]
        with pytest.raises((ExtractionFailed, UnsupportedMimeType)):
            ingest_from_codex(
                upload_id=uid,
                use_llm=False,
                dry_run=False,
                codex_db_url=codex_sqlite_url,
            )
        row = fake_codex_db.execute(
            'SELECT status, "errorMessage" FROM "Upload" WHERE id = ?',
            (uid,),
        ).fetchone()
        assert row["status"] == "failed", (
            f"{label}: expected status=failed, got {row['status']!r}"
        )
        assert fragment in (row["errorMessage"] or ""), (
            f"{label}: expected errorMessage to contain {fragment!r}, "
            f"got {row['errorMessage']!r}"
        )
    else:
        pytest.fail(f"unrecognised expected outcome: {expected!r}")

"""Status-transition invariant — keep the dashboard's pulse honest.

The Codex dashboard paints a different badge for each Upload status
(pending → extracting → awaiting_ingest → processing → ingested). If the
ingest pipeline ever skips one of those intermediate states, the badge
goes from "pending" straight to "ingested" and the founder loses all
mid-pipeline signal (am I stuck? which stage?).

The sqlite fixture's ``UploadStatusHistory`` trigger logs every UPDATE OF
``status``. These tests assert the logged sequence contains the expected
ordered subsequence — so any future refactor that drops an UPDATE fails
loudly rather than silently shipping a lying dashboard.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from noosphere.codex_bridge import ingest_from_codex
from noosphere.extractors import audio_extractor
from noosphere.extractors.base import ExtractedText


HAPPY_PATH_EXPECTED = ("extracting", "awaiting_ingest", "processing", "ingested")
FAILED_PATH_EXPECTED = ("extracting", "failed")


def _status_history(conn, upload_id: str) -> list[str]:
    return [
        row["status"]
        for row in conn.execute(
            'SELECT status FROM "UploadStatusHistory" '
            'WHERE upload_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC',
            (upload_id,),
        ).fetchall()
    ]


def _is_subsequence(needle: tuple[str, ...], haystack: list[str]) -> bool:
    """True if every element of ``needle`` appears in ``haystack`` in order
    (not necessarily contiguously)."""
    it = iter(haystack)
    return all(any(h == target for h in it) for target in needle)


def test_happy_path_emits_full_status_sequence(
    fake_codex_db, codex_sqlite_url, upload_factory, monkeypatch, tmp_path,
):
    """Audio happy path: extracting → awaiting_ingest → processing → ingested."""
    dummy = tmp_path / "talk.m4a"
    dummy.write_bytes(b"\x00\x00\x00\x20ftypM4A happy-path bytes")
    uid = upload_factory(
        mime="audio/mp4",
        text=None,
        file_path=str(dummy),
        file_size=dummy.stat().st_size,
    )

    def _fake_extract(self, content):
        return ExtractedText(
            text=(
                "the purpose of the school is inquiry not credentialing, "
                "and this sentence is long enough to clear the naive gate."
            ),
            source_format="audio/mp4",
            extraction_method="faster-whisper",
        )

    monkeypatch.setattr(audio_extractor.AudioExtractor, "extract", _fake_extract)

    ingest_from_codex(
        upload_id=uid,
        use_llm=False,
        dry_run=False,
        codex_db_url=codex_sqlite_url,
    )

    history = _status_history(fake_codex_db, uid)
    assert _is_subsequence(HAPPY_PATH_EXPECTED, history), (
        f"status history {history!r} is missing at least one of "
        f"{HAPPY_PATH_EXPECTED!r} — a skipped transition means the "
        "dashboard's pulse badge will lie to the founder."
    )
    # Terminal state must be 'ingested'.
    assert history[-1] == "ingested"


def test_unsupported_mime_path_emits_extracting_then_failed(
    fake_codex_db, codex_sqlite_url, upload_factory, tmp_path,
):
    """Failed path (unsupported MIME): extracting → failed. No processing /
    ingested should appear before the failure."""
    dummy = tmp_path / "bundle.zip"
    dummy.write_bytes(b"PK\x03\x04 dummy zip bytes")
    uid = upload_factory(
        mime="application/zip",
        text=None,
        file_path=str(dummy),
        file_size=dummy.stat().st_size,
    )

    with pytest.raises(Exception):
        ingest_from_codex(
            upload_id=uid,
            use_llm=False,
            dry_run=False,
            codex_db_url=codex_sqlite_url,
        )

    history = _status_history(fake_codex_db, uid)
    assert _is_subsequence(FAILED_PATH_EXPECTED, history), (
        f"status history {history!r} is missing {FAILED_PATH_EXPECTED!r}"
    )
    # The failure must land before any of the later lifecycle states —
    # a half-written 'processing' or 'ingested' on a failed row is worse
    # than silence.
    assert "processing" not in history, (
        f"unsupported-mime row must not transition through processing; "
        f"got history {history!r}"
    )
    assert "ingested" not in history
    assert history[-1] == "failed"


def test_text_only_upload_still_transitions_through_intermediate_states(
    fake_codex_db, codex_sqlite_url, upload_factory,
):
    """A text-only upload (textContent populated, no extractor work) should
    still cycle through extracting → awaiting_ingest → processing → ingested.
    Otherwise the dashboard's two-stage badge (extracting vs. processing)
    can never light up for the common-case row."""
    uid = upload_factory(
        mime="text/plain",
        text="a short note — no claims here.",
        file_path=None,
        original_name="note.txt",
    )

    ingest_from_codex(
        upload_id=uid,
        use_llm=False,
        dry_run=False,
        codex_db_url=codex_sqlite_url,
    )

    history = _status_history(fake_codex_db, uid)
    assert _is_subsequence(HAPPY_PATH_EXPECTED, history), (
        f"text upload status history {history!r} missing one of "
        f"{HAPPY_PATH_EXPECTED!r}"
    )

1	"""End-to-end smoke: real faster-whisper + claim extraction + DB writes.
2	
3	Gated behind ``NOOSPHERE_E2E_SMOKE=1`` so the default ``pytest -q`` run
4	never loads whisper weights (≈ 500 MB download, ≈ 2 min runtime).
5	
6	Generate the fixture once per developer machine with:
7	
8	    python noosphere/scripts/make_smoke_audio.py
9	
10	That script synthesizes a ~30 s .m4a clip via ``say`` + ffmpeg. The test
11	is skipped if the fixture isn't present so CI stays clean.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import os
17	from pathlib import Path
18	
19	import pytest
20	
21	
22	SMOKE_FIXTURE = (
23	    Path(__file__).parent.parent / "fixtures" / "smoke_meeting_30s.m4a"
24	)
25	
26	
27	@pytest.mark.skipif(
28	    os.environ.get("NOOSPHERE_E2E_SMOKE") != "1",
29	    reason="gated smoke; set NOOSPHERE_E2E_SMOKE=1 to enable",
30	)
31	def test_real_m4a_end_to_end(fake_codex_db, codex_sqlite_url, upload_factory):
32	    """Drives the full ingest pipeline against a real .m4a fixture:
33	    faster-whisper transcribes, the naive extractor pulls claims, and
34	    Conclusion rows land in the throwaway sqlite DB."""

"""End-to-end smoke: real faster-whisper + claim extraction + DB writes.

Gated behind ``NOOSPHERE_E2E_SMOKE=1`` so the default ``pytest -q`` run
never loads whisper weights (≈ 500 MB download, ≈ 2 min runtime).

Generate the fixture once per developer machine with:

    python noosphere/scripts/make_smoke_audio.py

That script synthesizes a ~30 s .m4a clip via ``say`` + ffmpeg. The test
is skipped if the fixture isn't present so CI stays clean.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
from pathlib import Path

import pytest


SMOKE_FIXTURE = (
    Path(__file__).parent.parent / "fixtures" / "smoke_meeting_30s.m4a"
)


@pytest.mark.skipif(
    os.environ.get("NOOSPHERE_E2E_SMOKE") != "1",
    reason="gated smoke; set NOOSPHERE_E2E_SMOKE=1 to enable",
)
def test_real_m4a_end_to_end(fake_codex_db, codex_sqlite_url, upload_factory):
    """Drives the full ingest pipeline against a real .m4a fixture:
    faster-whisper transcribes, the naive extractor pulls claims, and
    Conclusion rows land in the throwaway sqlite DB."""
    pytest.importorskip("faster_whisper")
    if not SMOKE_FIXTURE.exists():
        pytest.skip(
            f"{SMOKE_FIXTURE} not present (generate with "
            "scripts/make_smoke_audio.py)."
        )

    uid = upload_factory(
        mime="audio/mp4",
        text=None,
        file_path=str(SMOKE_FIXTURE),
        file_size=SMOKE_FIXTURE.stat().st_size,
        original_name="smoke_meeting_30s.m4a",
        title="Smoke meeting clip",
    )

    from noosphere.codex_bridge import ingest_from_codex

    result = ingest_from_codex(
        upload_id=uid,
        use_llm=False,
        dry_run=False,
        codex_db_url=codex_sqlite_url,
    )

    assert result.num_conclusions_written > 0, (
        "real transcription should yield at least one conclusion"
    )

    upload_row = fake_codex_db.execute(
        'SELECT status, "extractionMethod" FROM "Upload" WHERE id = ?',
        (uid,),
    ).fetchone()
    assert upload_row["status"] == "ingested"
    assert upload_row["extractionMethod"] == "faster-whisper"

    rows = fake_codex_db.execute(
        'SELECT text FROM "Conclusion" WHERE "organizationId" = ?',
        ("org_1",),
    ).fetchall()
    transcript_blob = " ".join(r["text"].lower() for r in rows)
    # Spot-check: the smoke-audio script (scripts/make_smoke_audio.py)
    # synthesizes a clip that mentions "purpose" — if this word is
    # missing from the transcribed conclusions, either the clip changed
    # or transcription silently degraded.
    assert "purpose" in transcript_blob, (
        "expected the word 'purpose' somewhere in transcribed conclusions "
        f"but got: {transcript_blob[:240]!r}"
    )

"""Shared pytest fixtures.

Two responsibilities:

1. Audio fixture synthesis — ``tiny_audio_fixture`` synthesizes a short
   English .m4a on demand (via macOS ``say`` + ffmpeg) rather than
   shipping bytes in git. Keeps the repo lean and sidesteps the
   "who owns this recording?" IP question. Cached under
   ``tests/fixtures/`` so repeat runs are free.

2. Codex DB factories — ``fake_codex_db`` spins up a throwaway SQLite
   instance seeded from ``fixtures/minimal_codex_schema.sql`` so the
   ingest pipeline can run without a live Postgres. ``upload_factory``
   inserts rows; ``sqlite_url_for`` turns a connection into the URL
   that ``ingest_from_codex(codex_db_url=...)`` expects.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import shutil
import sqlite3
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
from uuid import uuid4

import pytest


_FIXTURES_DIR = Path(__file__).parent / "fixtures"
_TINY_AUDIO = _FIXTURES_DIR / "tiny_audio.m4a"
_TINY_PHRASE = "Noosphere audio extractor test, one two three."
_SCHEMA_FILE = _FIXTURES_DIR / "minimal_codex_schema.sql"


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def tiny_audio_fixture() -> Path:
    """Return the path to a tiny .m4a clip, synthesizing on first use.

    Skips the calling test if neither ``say`` nor ``ffmpeg`` are on PATH.
    """
    if _TINY_AUDIO.exists() and _TINY_AUDIO.stat().st_size > 0:
        return _TINY_AUDIO

    say = shutil.which("say")
    ffmpeg = shutil.which("ffmpeg")
    if say is None or ffmpeg is None:
        pytest.skip(
            "tiny_audio fixture requires macOS `say` + ffmpeg to synthesize; "
            "install ffmpeg or ship a pre-recorded clip at "
            f"{_TINY_AUDIO} to enable."
        )

    _FIXTURES_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    aiff = _TINY_AUDIO.with_suffix(".aiff")
    try:
        subprocess.run(
            [say, "-o", str(aiff), "--data-format=LEI16@22050", _TINY_PHRASE],
            check=True, capture_output=True,
        )
        subprocess.run(
            [
                ffmpeg, "-hide_banner", "-loglevel", "error", "-y",
                "-i", str(aiff),
                "-ac", "1", "-ar", "16000", "-c:a", "aac", "-b:a", "48k",
                str(_TINY_AUDIO),
            ],
            check=True, capture_output=True,
        )
    finally:
        if aiff.exists():
            aiff.unlink()

    return _TINY_AUDIO


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Codex DB factories (SQLite-backed, test-only)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def _sqlite_path_of(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> str:
    """Return the on-disk path backing a sqlite connection's ``main`` DB."""
    for row in conn.execute("PRAGMA database_list").fetchall():
        # row: (seq, name, file) regardless of row_factory.
        name = row["name"] if isinstance(row, sqlite3.Row) else row[1]
        file_ = row["file"] if isinstance(row, sqlite3.Row) else row[2]
        if name == "main":
            return file_
    raise RuntimeError("could not derive sqlite path from connection")


@pytest.fixture
def fake_codex_db(tmp_path):
    """A throwaway SQLite DB seeded with the ingest-relevant Codex schema."""
    path = tmp_path / "codex.db"
    conn = sqlite3.connect(str(path))
    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
    conn.executescript(_SCHEMA_FILE.read_text())
    # Seed a minimal Organization row so the optional slug-filter check
    # (and any future referential-integrity probes) have something to find.
    conn.execute(
        'INSERT INTO "Organization" (id, slug, name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)',
        ("org_1", "test-org", "Test Org"),
    )
    conn.commit()
    try:
        yield conn
    finally:
        conn.close()


@pytest.fixture
def codex_sqlite_url(fake_codex_db) -> str:
    """The ``sqlite://`` URL that ``ingest_from_codex(codex_db_url=...)``
    understands (teaches the bridge to route through sqlite3 instead of
    psycopg2). Paired 1:1 with ``fake_codex_db``."""
    return f"sqlite://{_sqlite_path_of(fake_codex_db)}"


def _insert_upload(
    conn: sqlite3.Connection,
    *,
    mime: str,
    text: str | None = None,
    file_path: str | None = None,
    file_size: int = 0,
    original_name: str = "test",
    title: str = "test",
    org_id: str = "org_1",
    founder_id: str = "u_1",
) -> str:
    uid = f"cx_{uuid4().hex[:22]}"
    conn.execute(
        'INSERT INTO "Upload" '
        '(id, "organizationId", "founderId", title, "textContent", status, '
        ' "mimeType", "originalName", "filePath", "fileSize") '
        'VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)',
        (
            uid,
            org_id,
            founder_id,
            title,
            text,
            "pending",
            mime,
            original_name,
            file_path,
            file_size,
        ),
    )
    conn.commit()
    return uid


@pytest.fixture
def upload_factory(fake_codex_db):
    def _make(**kw) -> str:
        return _insert_upload(fake_codex_db, **kw)
    return _make


@pytest.fixture
def scratch_binary_fixture(tmp_path) -> Path:
    """A small on-disk file used when the ingest pipeline needs *some* bytes
    to fetch but the contents are irrelevant (e.g. extractor is stubbed,
    or the MIME is unsupported and dispatch fails before parsing)."""
    p = tmp_path / "scratch.bin"
    p.write_bytes(b"\x00\x01\x02 scratch payload")
    return p

  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE INDEX "Conclusion_org_norm_idx"
  ON "Conclusion" ("organizationId", "normalizedText");

CREATE TABLE "ConclusionSource" (
  "conclusionId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "uploadId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY ("conclusionId", "uploadId")
);

CREATE TABLE "Contradiction" (
  "id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  "organizationId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "claimAId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "claimBId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "severity" REAL NOT NULL,
  "sixLayerJson" TEXT,
  "narrative" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "sourceUploadId" TEXT,
  "status" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE "OpenQuestion" (
  "id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  "organizationId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "noosphereId" TEXT UNIQUE,
  "summary" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "claimAId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "claimBId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "unresolvedReason" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "layerDisagreementSummary" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "sourceUploadId" TEXT,
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE "ResearchSuggestion" (
  "id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  "organizationId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "noosphereId" TEXT UNIQUE,
  "title" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "summary" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "rationale" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "readingUris" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  "sessionLabel" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "suggestedForFounderId" TEXT,
  "sourceUploadId" TEXT,
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE "AuditEvent" (
  "id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  "organizationId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "founderId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "uploadId" TEXT,
  "action" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "detail" TEXT,
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Status-transition log. Populated by the trigger below so the ingest tests
-- can assert the ordered sequence (pending → extracting → awaiting_ingest →
-- processing → ingested) without instrumenting the pipeline itself.
CREATE TABLE "UploadStatusHistory" (
  "id" INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  "upload_id" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "status" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "ts" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TRIGGER "upload_status_log"
AFTER UPDATE OF "status" ON "Upload"
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO "UploadStatusHistory" ("upload_id", "status")
  VALUES (NEW."id", NEW."status");
END;

1	"""
2	Process Theseus Codex uploads locally.
3	
4	Story
5	-----
6	The Codex web app (on Vercel) accepts file uploads into its Postgres
7	database but cannot run the Noosphere Python pipeline in that runtime —
8	Vercel's serverless Node has no Python interpreter. Uploads land as
9	``queued_offline`` with their ``textContent`` sitting in the DB until
10	someone runs this command on a machine that DOES have Noosphere.
11	
12	What it does
13	------------
14	Given an Upload id, this module:
15	
16	1. Connects to the Codex's Postgres (the shared Supabase instance — same
17	   DB, different tables than Noosphere would normally write to).
18	2. Reads the ``Upload`` row, pulls ``textContent``.
19	3. Extracts atomic claims from the text. Two extraction modes:
20	     - default / naive: rule-based sentence splitter that keeps only
21	       assertoric-looking sentences (filters questions, fragments, mere
22	       listings). Zero dependencies beyond the standard library, works
23	       without an LLM API key.
24	     - ``--with-llm``: delegates to the registered ``extract_claims``
25	       method (the Noosphere LLM pipeline). Higher quality, requires
26	       ``ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`` or ``OPENAI_API_KEY`` via noosphere.config.
27	4. Writes each extracted claim back into the Codex's ``Conclusion``
28	   table (tier=``open``, confidence=0.5) so it appears in the Codex web
29	   UI immediately.
30	5. Updates the Upload row to ``status='ingested'`` and sets
31	   ``claimsCount`` so the dashboard counters reflect the work.
32	6. Writes an ``AuditEvent`` row for traceability.
33	
34	Why write to the Codex schema directly instead of Noosphere's own
35	-----------------------------------------------------------------
36	The goal of this command is "user uploaded a file, user sees something
37	in the Codex UI after running Noosphere locally". Writing to Noosphere's
38	separate tables would require the Codex UI to also query those tables
39	(the existing Python-spawn bridges do this, but they don't work on
40	Vercel either). Writing directly to the Codex's ``Conclusion`` table
41	gives the end-to-end "I uploaded, I can see analysis" flow without any
42	additional infrastructure. Full Noosphere-side artifact/chunk/claim
43	storage can be added later via ``--also-write-noosphere``.
44	"""
45	
46	from __future__ import annotations
47	
48	import json
49	import os
50	import re
51	import sys
52	import uuid
53	from dataclasses import dataclass
54	from datetime import datetime, timezone
55	from typing import Optional
56	
57	try:
58	    import psycopg2
59	    import psycopg2.extras
60	except ImportError as e:  # pragma: no cover
61	    raise RuntimeError(
62	        "psycopg2 is required for the Codex bridge. "
63	        "Install with: pip install psycopg2-binary"
64	    ) from e
65	
66	
67	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68	# SQLite shim — test-only codex connection.
69	#
70	# Prod always points at Supabase / Postgres. The ingest test suite spins up an
71	# in-process SQLite DB seeded from ``tests/fixtures/minimal_codex_schema.sql``
72	# so the pipeline SQL can be exercised without a running Postgres. The shim
73	# below is the cheapest adapter that lets the (otherwise psycopg2-shaped)
74	# pipeline run unchanged under either backend: translate ``%s`` → ``?``,
75	# yield dict-shaped rows, and expose the handful of cursor/connection methods
76	# the pipeline actually calls.
77	#
78	# Triggered by a ``sqlite://<path>`` URL; anything else goes to psycopg2.
79	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
80	
81	
82	class _SqliteCodexCursor:
83	    """Translate psycopg2-style calls to sqlite3 calls and return dict rows."""
84	
85	    def __init__(self, cur):
86	        self._cur = cur
87	
88	    @staticmethod
89	    def _translate(query: str) -> str:
90	        return query.replace("%s", "?")
91	
92	    def execute(self, query, params=()):
93	        self._cur.execute(self._translate(query), params or ())
94	        return self
95	
96	    def executemany(self, query, seq):
97	        self._cur.executemany(self._translate(query), seq)
98	        return self
99	
100	    def fetchone(self):
101	        row = self._cur.fetchone()
102	        return dict(row) if row is not None else None
103	
104	    def fetchall(self):
105	        return [dict(r) for r in self._cur.fetchall()]
106	
107	    def __iter__(self):
108	        for row in self._cur:
109	            yield dict(row)
110	
111	    @property
112	    def rowcount(self):
113	        return self._cur.rowcount
114	
115	    def close(self):
116	        self._cur.close()
117	
118	
119	class _SqliteCodexConn:
120	    def __init__(self, conn):
121	        self._conn = conn
122	
123	    def cursor(self, *_args, **_kwargs):
124	        return _SqliteCodexCursor(self._conn.cursor())
125	
126	    def commit(self):
127	        self._conn.commit()
128	
129	    def rollback(self):
130	        self._conn.rollback()
131	
132	    def close(self):
133	        self._conn.close()
134	
135	
136	def _open_codex_connection(url: str):
137	    """Open a Codex DB connection. ``sqlite://<path>`` triggers the test
138	    shim; anything else is treated as a Postgres URL for psycopg2."""
139	    if url.startswith("sqlite://"):
140	        import sqlite3
141	
142	        path = url[len("sqlite://"):]
143	        conn = sqlite3.connect(path)
144	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
145	        return _SqliteCodexConn(conn)
146	    return psycopg2.connect(url)
147	
148	
149	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
150	# Postgres-safe sanitization.
151	#
152	# Postgres's UTF-8 enforcement rejects strings containing the NUL
153	# byte (``\u0000``) with "invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8":
154	# 0x00". PDF extraction, DOCX extraction, and even LLM output
155	# occasionally produce them. Every string that's about to be inserted
156	# into the Codex DB flows through ``_db_safe`` first so one bad
157	# byte doesn't nuke the whole ingest.
158	#
159	# We also strip other C0 control chars (except tab/newline/CR) and
160	# lone UTF-16 surrogate halves, which behave the same way on some
161	# server collations.
162	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
163	
164	# C0 controls minus \t (\x09), \n (\x0a), \r (\x0d). Also nuke BOM.
165	_DB_UNSAFE = re.compile(r"[\x00-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\ufeff]")
166	_DB_SURROGATE = re.compile(r"[\ud800-\udfff]")
167	
168	
169	def _db_safe(s: Optional[str], *, cap: Optional[int] = None) -> str:
170	    """Scrub for Postgres and optionally cap length.
171	
172	    ``None`` / non-string inputs return ``""`` so callers can unconditionally
173	    pass field values without null-checking first.
174	    """
175	    if s is None:
176	        return ""
177	    text = str(s)
178	    text = _DB_UNSAFE.sub("", text)
179	    text = _DB_SURROGATE.sub("", text)
180	    if cap is not None and len(text) > cap:
181	        text = text[:cap]
182	    return text
183	
184	
185	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
186	# Conclusion dedup — normalised text form.
187	#
188	# The Codex DB has a `Conclusion.normalizedText` column populated by this
189	# helper and matched by a `(organizationId, normalizedText)` index. Any
190	# time this pipeline is about to write a new Conclusion we first look up
191	# an existing row with the same normalised key in the same org; if one
192	# exists we skip the INSERT and add this upload as an extra source via
193	# `ConclusionSource`. That's what makes deletion work correctly — a
194	# Conclusion whose one-and-only source is removed gets orphan-deleted,
195	# while a Conclusion supported by several uploads stays in place with
196	# the surviving uploads as its remaining sources.
197	#
198	# The formula (lower + strip + collapse whitespace) matches the SQL
199	# backfill in
200	# `prisma/migrations/20260420000000_conclusion_sources_and_derived_cascade`,
201	# so pre-existing rows and freshly-inserted rows share the same key.
202	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
203	
204	_WS_COLLAPSE_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
205	
206	
207	def _normalize_claim_text(text: str) -> str:
208	    """Return the dedup key for a claim. Empty input → empty key."""
209	    if not text:
210	        return ""
211	    return _WS_COLLAPSE_RE.sub(" ", text).strip().lower()
212	
213	
214	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
215	# Naive claim extraction — works without an LLM. Conservative: keeps only
216	# sentences that look like first-person / declarative assertions, filters
217	# questions and fragments. Good enough for a first-pass Codex display.
218	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
219	
220	
221	# Sentence-level tokens ending with a period / exclamation / line break.
222	# Non-greedy so we don't over-fuse sentences across paragraph breaks.
223	_SENT_RE = re.compile(r"[^.!?\n]+[.!?]+|[^.!?\n]+(?=\n|$)", re.MULTILINE)
224	
225	# Heuristic filters for sentences we SHOULDN'T promote to claims.
226	_QUESTION_RE = re.compile(r"\?\s*$")
227	_LIST_BULLET_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*[-*•\d]+[\.\)]?\s+")
228	_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://|www\.")
229	_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"^#+\s|^={3,}\s*$|^-{3,}\s*$")
230	
231	
232	@dataclass
233	class NaiveClaim:
234	    text: str
235	    claim_type: str = "empirical"  # conservative default
236	
237	
238	def naive_extract_claims(text: str, *, max_claims: int = 40) -> list[NaiveClaim]:
239	    """
240	    Sentence-split a blob of text and keep only sentences that look like
241	    substantive claims:
242	      * length ≥ 40 chars (filters "Yes.", "Ok.", lone bullets)
243	      * length ≤ 400 chars (filters run-on paragraphs that aren't atomic)
244	      * not a question (``?`` at end)
245	      * not a list bullet / markdown header
246	      * contains at least one verb-like word (``is|are|was|were|should|must|can|may|will``)
247	        OR a contextual connector (``because|therefore|given|since``)
248	
249	    Returns at most ``max_claims`` so a 50-page transcript doesn't blow
250	    up the Codex with thousands of rows.
251	    """
252	    out: list[NaiveClaim] = []
253	    cleaned = _HEADER_RE.sub("", text)
254	    # Normalise whitespace so sentence splitting doesn't fragment on line
255	    # wraps inside a paragraph. We preserve paragraph breaks (double-newline)
256	    # because they are a useful signal for separating assertions; we just
257	    # rejoin line wraps WITHIN a paragraph into spaces.
258	    cleaned = re.sub(r"(?<!\n)\n(?!\n)", " ", cleaned)
259	    cleaned = re.sub(r"[ \t]+", " ", cleaned)
260	    # Walk sentences.
261	    for match in _SENT_RE.finditer(cleaned):
262	        raw = match.group(0).strip()
263	        if not raw:
264	            continue
265	        if len(raw) < 40 or len(raw) > 400:
266	            continue
267	        if _QUESTION_RE.search(raw):
268	            continue
269	        if _LIST_BULLET_RE.match(raw):
270	            # Strip the bullet and re-check length.
271	            stripped = _LIST_BULLET_RE.sub("", raw).strip()
272	            if len(stripped) < 40 or len(stripped) > 400:
273	                continue
274	            raw = stripped
275	        if _URL_RE.search(raw):
276	            # Drop sentences that are mostly URLs — they're rarely claims.
277	            continue
278	        lower = raw.lower()
279	        has_verb = bool(
280	            re.search(
281	                r"\b(is|are|was|were|should|must|can|may|will|would|do|does|did|have|has|had|seems|appears|means|implies|suggests|shows|proves|causes|requires|matters)\b",
282	                lower,
283	            )
284	        )
285	        has_connector = bool(
286	            re.search(r"\b(because|therefore|thus|hence|since|given that)\b", lower)
287	        )
288	        if not (has_verb or has_connector):
289	            continue
290	        # Classify: rough taxonomy based on vocabulary.
291	        claim_type = "empirical"
292	        if re.search(r"\b(should|must|ought|right|wrong|better|worse|good|bad)\b", lower):
293	            claim_type = "normative"
294	        elif re.search(
295	            r"\b(method|framework|approach|process|procedure|methodology)\b", lower
296	        ):
297	            claim_type = "methodological"
298	        elif re.search(r"\b(will|won't|shall|going to|expect|predict)\b", lower):
299	            claim_type = "predictive"
300	        out.append(NaiveClaim(text=raw, claim_type=claim_type))
301	        if len(out) >= max_claims:
302	            break
303	    return out
304	
305	
306	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
307	# LLM-backed extraction — uses Noosphere's registered ``extract_claims`` method.
308	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
309	
310	
311	def llm_extract_claims(
312	    text: str,
313	    *,
314	    chunk_char_size: int = 3500,
315	    max_chunks: int = 10,
316	    upload_id: str = "upload",
317	) -> list[NaiveClaim]:
318	    """LLM-backed extraction via Noosphere's ``extract_claims`` method.
319	
320	    Raises if the LLM is unconfigured — the caller is expected to have
321	    passed --with-llm deliberately.
322	    """
323	    from noosphere.methods.extract_claims import (
324	        ExtractClaimsInput,
325	        extract_claims,
326	    )
327	
328	    # Chunk the text so no single LLM call blows past the context budget.
329	    # Paragraph splits preferred; fall back to hard-cut if a paragraph is
330	    # itself huge.
331	    raw_paras = re.split(r"\n\s*\n+", text)
332	    chunks: list[str] = []
333	    buf: list[str] = []
334	    size = 0
335	    for p in raw_paras:
336	        if size + len(p) > chunk_char_size and buf:
337	            chunks.append("\n\n".join(buf))
338	            buf = []
339	            size = 0
340	        if len(p) > chunk_char_size:
341	            # Hard-split the monster paragraph.
342	            for i in range(0, len(p), chunk_char_size):
343	                chunks.append(p[i : i + chunk_char_size])
344	        else:
345	            buf.append(p)
346	            size += len(p) + 2
347	    if buf:
348	        chunks.append("\n\n".join(buf))
349	
350	    chunks = chunks[:max_chunks]
351	
352	    out: list[NaiveClaim] = []
353	    for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
354	        result = extract_claims(
355	            ExtractClaimsInput(
356	                chunk_text=chunk,
357	                chunk_id=f"{upload_id}_chunk_{i}",
358	                episode_id=upload_id,
359	                speaker_name="founder",
360	                speaker_role="founder",
361	            )
362	        )
363	        for c in result.claims:
364	            if not c.text.strip():
365	                continue
366	            # Drop claims the LLM flagged as external (interview prompts,
367	            # counter-positions, quoted views) so they never land in the
368	            # founder's Conclusion canon. This is defense-in-depth: the
369	            # extractor already labels them, the bridge refuses to
370	            # propagate them.
371	            if getattr(c, "is_author_assertion", True) is False:
372	                continue
373	            out.append(NaiveClaim(text=c.text.strip(), claim_type=c.claim_type))
374	    return out
375	
376	
377	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
378	# Core pipeline — connects to Codex Postgres, reads upload, writes results.
379	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
380	
381	
382	@dataclass
383	class IngestFromCodexResult:
384	    upload_id: str
385	    title: str
386	    num_claims_extracted: int
387	    num_conclusions_written: int
388	    num_contradictions_written: int
389	    num_open_questions_written: int
390	    num_research_suggestions_written: int
391	    dry_run: bool
392	    mode: str  # "naive" | "llm"
393	
394	
395	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
396	# Contradiction detection — naive (always runs) + LLM-augmented (optional).
397	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
398	
399	
400	@dataclass
401	class DetectedContradiction:
402	    """A contradicting claim pair. `a_idx` / `b_idx` refer to indices in
403	    the claim list we just extracted; if one side is an existing firm
404	    Conclusion (cross-upload contradiction) we use `b_existing_id` instead
405	    of `b_idx`."""
406	    a_idx: int
407	    b_idx: int | None  # None when b is an existing firm Conclusion
408	    b_existing_id: str | None  # Conclusion.id when b is from the firm corpus
409	    severity: float   # 0..1
410	    narrative: str
411	    source: str       # "heuristic" | "llm"
412	
413	
414	_NEGATION_TOKENS = re.compile(
415	    r"\b(not|never|no|none|nothing|nobody|nowhere|n'?t|cannot|can'?t|won'?t|"
416	    r"shouldn'?t|wouldn'?t|couldn'?t|isn'?t|aren'?t|wasn'?t|weren'?t|"
417	    r"doesn'?t|didn'?t|don'?t|haven'?t|hasn'?t|hadn'?t)\b",
418	    flags=re.IGNORECASE,
419	)
420	_STOPWORDS = {
421	    "the", "a", "an", "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being",
422	    "and", "or", "but", "if", "then", "of", "in", "on", "at", "to", "for",
423	    "by", "with", "from", "as", "that", "this", "these", "those", "it",
424	    "its", "we", "our", "you", "they", "them", "their", "i", "me", "my",
425	    "he", "she", "his", "her", "has", "have", "had", "do", "does", "did",
426	    "will", "would", "should", "could", "can", "may", "must", "ought",
427	    "so", "than", "such", "some", "any", "all", "every", "each", "both",
428	    "more", "most", "much", "many", "few",
429	}
430	
431	
432	def _content_tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
433	    toks = re.findall(r"\b[a-z][a-z']+\b", text.lower())
434	    return {t for t in toks if t not in _STOPWORDS and len(t) >= 4}
435	
436	
437	def _has_negation(text: str) -> bool:
438	    return bool(_NEGATION_TOKENS.search(text))
439	
440	
441	def naive_detect_contradictions(
442	    claims: list[NaiveClaim],
443	) -> list[DetectedContradiction]:
444	    """
445	    Heuristic pairwise contradiction scan on the new claims. Flags pairs
446	    where:
447	      (1) their content vocabulary overlaps significantly (same subject), AND
448	      (2) exactly one contains a negation marker (opposite polarity).
449	
450	    This catches the obvious "X should always …" vs "X should not …" case
451	    without needing an NLI model. Recall is modest; precision is decent
452	    enough that the flagged pairs are usually worth a human's attention.
453	    """
454	    out: list[DetectedContradiction] = []
455	    tok_sets = [_content_tokens(c.text) for c in claims]
456	    neg_flags = [_has_negation(c.text) for c in claims]
457	    for i in range(len(claims)):
458	        for j in range(i + 1, len(claims)):
459	            if neg_flags[i] == neg_flags[j]:
460	                continue  # both affirmative or both negative — no polarity flip
461	            if not tok_sets[i] or not tok_sets[j]:
462	                continue
463	            shared = tok_sets[i] & tok_sets[j]
464	            # Require meaningful overlap — otherwise it's just two unrelated
465	            # sentences that happen to have different polarity. A Jaccard
466	            # of 0.35 on content tokens is a useful middle ground.
467	            smaller = min(len(tok_sets[i]), len(tok_sets[j]))
468	            if smaller == 0 or (len(shared) / smaller) < 0.35:
469	                continue
470	            # Severity proxy: how much do they overlap? More overlap = more
471	            # directly contradictory.
472	            overlap_ratio = len(shared) / smaller
473	            severity = min(0.9, 0.4 + overlap_ratio * 0.5)
474	            out.append(
475	                DetectedContradiction(
476	                    a_idx=i,
477	                    b_idx=j,
478	                    b_existing_id=None,
479	                    severity=severity,
480	                    narrative=(
481	                        f"Heuristic: shared vocabulary {sorted(shared)[:6]} "
482	                        f"but opposite polarity."
483	                    ),
484	                    source="heuristic",
485	                )
486	            )
487	    return out
488	
489	
490	def llm_detect_contradictions_and_questions(
491	    claims: list[NaiveClaim],
492	    firm_conclusions: list[tuple[str, str]],  # (id, text) of existing firm claims
493	    *,
494	    upload_title: str,
495	) -> dict:
496	    """
497	    Single LLM call that does all three analysis tasks at once —
498	    contradictions, open questions, research suggestions. Putting them in
499	    one prompt is:
500	      (a) cheaper (single context load);
501	      (b) more coherent — the questions are generated in light of the
502	          contradictions the LLM just identified, instead of being
503	          independently invented.
504	
505	    Returns a dict shaped like:
506	      {
507	        "contradictions": [{"a": int, "b": int, "severity": float, "narrative": str}],
508	        "cross_contradictions": [{"a": int, "existing_id": str, "severity": float, "narrative": str}],
509	        "open_questions": [{"summary": str, "unresolved_reason": str, "a": int, "b": int|None}],
510	        "research_suggestions": [{"title": str, "summary": str, "rationale": str}],
511	      }
512	    Missing keys are backfilled to empty lists so the caller never
513	    explodes on a partial response.
514	
515	    Raises on LLM failure so the caller can decide whether to fall back
516	    to heuristic-only mode.
517	    """
518	    from noosphere.llm import llm_client_from_settings
519	
520	    llm = llm_client_from_settings()
521	
522	    new_lines = "\n".join(
523	        f"  [{i}] {c.text}" for i, c in enumerate(claims)
524	    )
525	    firm_lines = (
526	        "\n".join(f"  <{fid[:10]}> {txt[:240]}" for fid, txt in firm_conclusions[:40])
527	        if firm_conclusions
528	        else "  (none yet — this is the first upload for this firm.)"
529	    )
530	
531	    system = (
532	        "You are an analyst helping a firm surface contradictions, open "
533	        "questions, and research directions from a newly uploaded document. "
534	        "Answer ONLY in JSON matching the schema below. Be conservative — "
535	        "only flag genuine contradictions, not mere topical overlap. Only "
536	        "propose questions a founder could actually research. Never "
537	        "fabricate evidence."
538	    )
539	    user = f"""New document: "{upload_title}"
540	
541	CLAIMS extracted from this document (indices in brackets):
542	{new_lines}
543	
544	FIRM'S EXISTING CONCLUSIONS (for cross-upload contradiction detection):
545	{firm_lines}
546	
547	Return a single JSON object with these keys:
548	{{
549	  "contradictions": [
550	    {{"a": <idx>, "b": <idx>, "severity": <0..1>, "narrative": "<≤200 chars, why they conflict>"}}
551	  ],
552	  "cross_contradictions": [
553	    {{"a": <idx>, "existing_id": "<existing firm claim id prefix>", "severity": <0..1>, "narrative": "<≤200 chars>"}}
554	  ],
555	  "open_questions": [
556	    {{"summary": "<≤180 chars, a concrete question>", "unresolved_reason": "<why unresolved>", "a": <idx or null>, "b": <idx or null>}}
557	  ],
558	  "research_suggestions": [
559	    {{"title": "<short title>", "summary": "<one sentence>", "rationale": "<why this matters>"}}
560	  ]
561	}}
562	
563	Rules:
564	- 0-5 contradictions, 0-3 cross_contradictions, 3-6 open_questions, 2-4 research_suggestions.
565	- If no real contradictions exist, return [] for that list. Do not pad.
566	- `a` and `b` must be valid indices into the CLAIMS list above.
567	- Output ONLY the JSON object, no prose."""
568	
569	    raw = llm.complete(system=system, user=user, max_tokens=2500)
570	    m = re.search(r"\{.*\}", raw, re.DOTALL)
571	    if not m:
572	        raise RuntimeError(f"LLM returned non-JSON: {raw[:200]}")
573	    payload = json.loads(m.group(0))
574	    return {
575	        "contradictions": payload.get("contradictions", []) or [],
576	        "cross_contradictions": payload.get("cross_contradictions", []) or [],
577	        "open_questions": payload.get("open_questions", []) or [],
578	        "research_suggestions": payload.get("research_suggestions", []) or [],
579	    }
